title: Plato's Theory of Forms
description: Plato's doctrine that non-material abstract forms represent the most accurate reality.
definition: The theory posits that the physical world is not the real world; instead, ultimate reality exists beyond our physical world in a realm of abstract, perfect, unchanging Forms or Ideas (e.g., the Form of Beauty, Justice, or Equality). Physical objects are imperfect copies or imitations of these perfect Forms, known only through reason, not sensory experience.

proofs.description: Developed in Platonic dialogues such as Phaedo, Republic, and Symposium.
The 'One over Many' argument: for any group of similar things, there is a single Form that they all share.
